skill.name: typegpu
skill.description: TypeGPU and raw WebGPU adapter patterns for HyperFrames. Use when creating GPU-rendered compositions with TypeGPU, raw WebGPU, WGSL fragment shaders, compute pipelines, liquid glass effects, particle systems, or any canvas layer driven by navigator.gpu that responds to HyperFrames hf-seek events.
skill.summary: Guides deterministic TypeGPU and raw WebGPU rendering inside HyperFrames compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and blocker patterns, but review found them inside Markdown inline code, browser JavaScript examples, and WGSL shader snippets. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential access, shell execution, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick matches are Markdown inline code and fenced examples in SKILL.md. They document WebGPU browser APIs and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown guide, and the matched backticks delimit code spans or code fences. No shell language or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keyword Matches Are WebGPU and WGSL Terms
low_findings.description: The high-risk keyword matches occur in WGSL shader examples and WebGPU rendering guidance. They are not command-and-control instructions, cryptography code, or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear inside shader syntax such as vec2f and SDF helper functions. Semantic review found no network destinations, encoded payloads, or host reconnaissance logic.

content.value_statement: GPU-rendered HyperFrames compositions need deterministic timing and capture-safe rendering. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete TypeGPU, WebGPU, and WGSL patterns for frame-accurate effects.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to initialize WebGPU for HyperFrames canvas layers.
Shows how to render from hf-seek time instead of wall-clock time.
Documents synchronous GSAP timeline registration before async GPU setup.
Provides patterns for video-backed textures and liquid glass effects.
Includes WGSL snippets for full-screen triangles and rounded-rect SDF masks.
Covers capture-safe GPU queue flushing for video renders.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not provide a runnable project scaffold.
It assumes the browser and renderer support WebGPU.
It does not implement complete TypeGPU pipelines or bind groups.
Headless Chrome video texture capture may require pre-extracted PNG frames.

use_cases.title: Create GPU Motion Graphics
use_cases.description: Build WebGPU canvas layers that stay synchronized with HyperFrames timeline seeks.

use_cases.title: Add Liquid Glass Effects
use_cases.description: Design video-backed distortion, blur, and chromatic refraction effects for rendered compositions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Deterministic Renders
use_cases.description: Avoid nondeterministic clocks and animation loops when exporting exact video frames.

prompt_templates.title: Start a WebGPU Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typegpu skill to outline a minimal HyperFrames WebGPU canvas layer that renders on hf-seek events.

prompt_templates.title: Synchronize Timeline Elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typegpu skill to combine synchronous GSAP caption tweens with async WebGPU initialization.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Video Effect
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typegpu skill to plan a liquid glass effect that samples a video texture and renders deterministically.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Render Determinism
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typegpu skill to review my HyperFrames GPU composition for timing, frame capture, and WebGPU fallback issues.

output_examples.input: Create a deterministic GPU background for a HyperFrames composition.
output_examples.output: The response describes WebGPU initialization, hf-seek event rendering, timeline registration order, and queue flushing before capture.

output_examples.input: Make a liquid glass pill over a video layer.
output_examples.output: The response proposes a downsample blur pass, a rounded SDF mask, native video texture sizing, and production render caveats.

output_examples.input: Review my GPU animation for export problems.
output_examples.output: The response flags wall-clock timing, requestAnimationFrame loops, missing WebGPU guards, and unflushed GPU submissions.

best_practices: Register HyperFrames timelines synchronously before awaiting WebGPU setup.
Render from hf-seek time or window.__hfTypegpuTime for exact frame output.
Flush submitted GPU work before frame capture in render mode.
anti_patterns: Do not drive exported animation with performance.now.
Do not rely on requestAnimationFrame as the render loop.
Do not assume video textures work reliably in headless Chrome renders.

faq.question: Does this skill require TypeGPU?
faq.answer: No. It covers both TypeGPU-style patterns and raw WebGPU adapter usage.

faq.question: Can it create complete shader pipelines?
faq.answer: It provides patterns and snippets, but you must supply project-specific pipelines and bind groups.

faq.question: Why must GSAP timelines be registered synchronously?
faq.answer: The HyperFrames player reads timelines at page load, before async WebGPU initialization completes.

faq.question: How does it keep renders deterministic?
faq.answer: It renders from HyperFrames seek time and avoids wall-clock animation sources.

faq.question: Does it handle browsers without WebGPU?
faq.answer: It reminds agents to check navigator.gpu and provide a fallback or early return.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed content is documentation with code examples and no confirmed malicious behavior.
